calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Of An Amended Order For Distribution
Off Calendar Sep-07-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number 266948 The Estate of Roger Preston Kampen.

Appearances: Bruce Feder for Chandler Flickinger. Charles Wolff for San Francisco Opera Association. Kevin Solan for Great America Insurance Company.

Mr. Feder stated that he had a proposed order for the Kampen matter that was signed off by all Parties but that Mr. Wollf had an issue with the Ellington order. Mr. Wolff stated that the Kampen order adequately protects his Client's interest with respect to a possible appeal, that there was an issue with the Ellington order, that he had no problem with the text drafted by Mr. Feder, but that he was uncomfortable with the power of the Court with entering an amended order and therefore he did not sign the Ellington order. Both matters were taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Cabrera.

MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number 266948 Estate of Roger Preston Kampen.

Appearances: Thomas Latham for The San Francisco Opera Association. Kevin Solan for Great American Assurity. Bruce Feder for Chandler Flickinger.

Mr. Latham informed the Court that mediation was unsuccessful, that the matter needed to be set for trial (4 hours) and that some discovery needed to be conducted. Both matters were continued to May 7, 2010 at 9:30 a.m., in Dept. 204 for trial on the surcharge issue.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

Off Calendar Nov-24-2009 Text Ruling

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Case Management Conference And Determination Of Mediation
Off Calendar Nov-24-2009 Continued To Feb-02-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number 266948 the Estate of Roger Preston Kampen.

Appearances: Thomas Latham for San Francisco Opera House. Rowena Navia for Chandler Flickinger. Kevin Solan for Great American Assurity.

Mr. Latham requested a case management conference be set in the first week of February and that the December 18, 2009 evidentiary hearing date be vacated. In addition, he stated that discovery was needed and that he would like to attend mediation prior to February. Furthermore, he stated that a $100,000.00 preliminary distribution would be appropriate and that he would prepare a stipulation with regard to said distribution. Ms. Navia stated that she would present it to her Client and that she was not willing to stipulate to anything at this point. Mr. Solan stated that the responses to the interrogatory questions were due in December, that he needed to take depositions and that discovery would be completed by January. The Court will sign the order referring the matters to pro bono mediation. Mediation is to be completed by January 26, 2010. Counsel is to inform the Examiner as to the outcome of mediation. Both matters were continued to February 2, 2010. An evidentiary hearing will be set on that date if needed. The December 18, 2009 evidentiary hearing date was vacated.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

Petition To Surcharge Administrator For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty And For An Accounting
Off Calendar Nov-24-2009 Continued To Feb-02-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Evidentiary Hearing On The Petition For Surcharge
Off Calendar Sep-21-2009 Continued To Dec-18-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Surcharge Administrator For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty And For An Accounting
Off Calendar Sep-21-2009 Continued To Nov-24-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number 266948 Estate of Roger Preston Kampen.

Appearances: Rowena Navia for Chandler Flickinger. Kevin Solan for Great American Insurance Co. Thomas Latham for The San Francisco Opera Association.

Ms. Navia stated that she had been contacted on Friday, that she had spoken to opposing Counsel and requested a 30 day continuance. The Court suggested mediation. Ms. Navia stated that she thought her Client would not object to mediation. Mr. Latham stated that he did not object and Mr. Solan stated that mediation was fine as long as it was done after the accounting was filed. The Court stated that it was debatable as to how you construe the damages and that it was unclear as to what the judicial decision would be. The accounting on the Kampen matter was moved from October 13, 2009 to November 24, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. A case management conference on both matters will also be held on November 24, 2009 and the Parties are to make a determination regarding mediation. The evidentiary hearings on calendar for October 23, 2009 for both matters were continued to December 18, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

Petition To Surcharge Administrator For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty And For An Accounting
Off Calendar Aug-04-2009 Continued To Sep-21-09 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Surcharge Administrator For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty And For An Accounting
Off Calendar Jul-21-2009 Continued To Sep-16-09 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: This matter was heard with case number 266948 Estate of Roger Preston Kampen.

Appearances: Kevin Solan for Assurity Great American Insurance Company. Charles Wolff for The San Francisco Opera Association (Petitioner). Chandler Flickinger appeared by telephone in Propria Persona.

Mr. Wolff stated that an informal accounting was presented to him, that the accounting appeared to be sufficiently in order, that there were a couple of discrete points/issues that should be entered in the surcharge petitions and that discovery needed to be conducted. Furthermore, Mr. Wolff stated that he would file papers with regard to what would be subject to the surcharges by August 11, 2009. An evidentiary hearing on the surcharge issues for both matters was set for October 23, 2009 at 9:00 a.m. With regard to the Kampen matter, Mr. Solan stated that the Inventory and Appraisal was filed this morning and that the accounting would be filed by July 31, 2009. Mr. Wolff stated that the Ellington matter was ready to be closed except for the surcharge issue. A status/case management conference on both matters was set for September 16, 2009 at 10:30 a.m. Mr. Flickinger will appear by telephone/court call. Counsel are to submit a joint case management brief by September 11, 2009 to the court. Mr. Wolff will prepare an order after hearing.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Jacqueline Chan, CSR#10276. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

Petition To Surcharge Administrator For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty And For An Accounting
Off Calendar Jul-08-2009 Continued To Jul-21-09 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Surcharge Administrator For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty And For An Accounting
Off Calendar May-06-2009 Continued To Jul-08-09 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Arthur Park and Kevin Solan for Great American Insurance, Company. Charles Wollff for the San Francisco Opera Association.

The Parties confirmed that they received copies of the supplemental account, the status report regarding the Great Western/Washington Mutual IRA accounts and copies of the bank statements. Mr. Flickinger stated that he believed that no other accounts existed on the Ellington matter. Mr. Wolff stated that he was not prepared to acknowledge receipt of all bank statements, that he wished to pursue the surcharge petition, that he could not confirm that all of the assets had been distributed until he was able to see an accounting and that he wanted to confirm that the IRA accounts were taken out of the Ellington estate and put into the Kampen estate. Mr. Solan stated that the supplemental and the amended account fully accounted for the Ellington estate. In addition, he stated that some of the bank statements were missing from the Kampen estate (case #266948) and that they needed more time to complete the accounting on that matter. The Petitions To Surcharge Administrator on the Ellington estate and the Kampen estate were continued to July 8, 2009 at 10:30 a.m, in Department 204 for status on trial setting. Mr. Flickinger is to file the accounting petition on the Kampen estate by June 23, 2009 with a hearing date of July 8, 2009 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Flickinger will appear by telephone.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Ann Mendoza, CSR#6812. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Jackson.

Petition To Surcharge Administrator For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty And For An Accounting
Off Calendar Mar-24-2009 Continued To May-06-09 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Chandler Flickenger appeared by telephone in Propria Persona. Charles Wolff for the San Francisco Opera Association. Arthur Park for Great America Insurance Company.

Mr. Flickinger explained the reasons for the delay in distributing the assets, informed the Court that the remaining assets were in two IRA accounts with Washington Mutual that may have been escheated to the state and that he was looking into this issue. Mr. Flickinger will file a substitution of Attorney. Mr. Wolff stated that a surcharge was appropriate and Mr. Park agreed that an accounting was appropriate. The Court directed Mr. Flickinger to file a supplemental accounting with all of the bank statments from 1999 to the current date with generated interest, to identify all accounts held and dispositions of all accounts including those referred to in previously filed accountings, and to provide copies of all of the bank statments from 1999 to curent to Mr. Wolff and Mr. Park. In addition, the Court directed Mr. Flickinger to file a report as to the process of recovering the IRA accounts from Washington Mutual, their legal position as to his demand for the accounts and the amount of interest earned to date. The matter was continued to May 6, 2009 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on all issues. Mr. Flickinger is to file the supplemental accounting by April 16, 2009 with a hearing date of May 6, 2009. The copies of all of the bank statements are due to opposing Counsel by April 29, 2009.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.
